headlines.text: US strikes Iranian missile sites and boats near Strait of Hormuz, calls it self-defense; IRGC says it downed a Reaper drone
Iran nuclear deal stalls as Rubio says disputes over wording are holding up agreement
Russia fires 100+ drones and two ballistic missiles at Ukraine overnight; Kremlin warns of major Kyiv barrage
Micron surges 18% as UBS triples price target; stock crosses $1 trillion market cap for first time
Gas prices hit four-year high at $4.56 national average for Memorial Day weekend
Stephen Colbert says goodbye: Late Show finale draws 6.74 million viewers with Paul McCartney as final guest
Euphoria Season 3 kills off Nate Jacobs in penultimate episode; finale set for May 31
ActBlue in full meltdown: DOJ circling, most of board and all lawyers gone amid foreign donation probe
Iran internet partially restored after longest nationwide shutdown in modern history -- 88 days
Supreme Court voting rights ruling could cost 19 Black lawmakers their seats, Congressional Black Caucus warns

breaking.text: IRGC claims it shot down a US Reaper drone over Iranian airspace as peace talks continue in Doha

stories.hed: US Strikes Iran, Iran Shoots Down Drone, Both Sides Still Technically Talking
stories.dek: The US military hit Iranian missile launch sites and mine-laying vessels near the Strait of Hormuz in what it called 'self-defense strikes,' while the IRGC responded by claiming it downed a US Reaper drone. Meanwhile, negotiators from Tehran are in Doha chatting with Qatari mediators about a peace deal -- because nothing says 'ceasefire' like simultaneous airstrikes and drone shootdowns.

stories.hed: Russia Fires 100+ Drones at Ukraine Overnight, Warns Kyiv to Brace for Worse
stories.dek: Russia launched two Iskander-M ballistic missiles and 122 drones at Ukraine overnight, killing 6 and injuring over 100, while Moscow told foreign nationals to leave Kyiv ahead of threatened strikes on 'decision-making centers.' Lavrov called Rubio to suggest the US evacuate its Kyiv diplomats, which is either a courtesy or a threat depending on your mood.

stories.hed: ActBlue Implodes: Board Gone, Lawyers Gone, DOJ Waiting in the Wings
stories.dek: The Democratic Party's main small-dollar fundraising platform has lost most of its board and its entire legal team amid a DOJ investigation into alleged foreign donations and misleading statements to Congress. Five deposed ActBlue employees took the Fifth Amendment a combined 146 times, which is not the kind of fundraising enthusiasm Democrats were hoping for heading into the midterms.

stories.hed: Supreme Court Voting Rights Ruling Could Oust 19 Black Lawmakers, CBC Says
stories.dek: The Supreme Court's latest blow to the Voting Rights Act could cost 19 Black lawmakers their seats over the next few years, according to the Congressional Black Caucus, which says it is fighting back. The ruling follows the court recently weakening the VRA while also ducking a related case that could limit enforcement of remaining minority voter protections.

stories.hed: Trump Torches His Own Cabinet: Bondi, Noem, and Chavez-DeRemer Out; Gabbard Next
stories.dek: Attorney General Pamela Bondi, DHS Secretary Kristi Noem, and Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer have all departed, with DNI Tulsi Gabbard reportedly on her way out next month -- making Trump's second-term Cabinet resemble a revolving door at a particularly dramatic hotel. Trump is hosting his first Cabinet meeting since March at Camp David on Wednesday, presumably to meet whichever new faces have survived the week.

markets.context: Markets surged on Iran peace deal optimism and a Micron AI blowout, while oil tanked on hopes the Strait of Hormuz might actually reopen someday.

gas.note: Gas hit a four-year high for Memorial Day -- your road trip to 'save money by driving' has aged poorly.

popCulture.hed: Stephen Colbert Signs Off: Late Show Finale Draws 6.74 Million Viewers with Paul McCartney, Bryan Cranston, and a Literal Wormhole
popCulture.dek: The Late Show with Stephen Colbert aired its final episode May 21 after 11 years and 1,801 episodes, with Paul McCartney as the surprise final guest and a surreal finale bit involving a wormhole consuming the Ed Sullivan Theater. The show -- canceled by CBS amid the Paramount-Skydance merger -- went out as the most-watched weeknight episode in its history, because of course it did.

genZ.definition: A viral TikTok audio format ripped from The Devil Wears Prada 2 (in theaters May 1), where creators film two contrasting shots -- one polished and correct, one chaotic and wrong -- with Miranda Priestly's withering dismissal as the punchline. It exploded after the film's release and has become May's defining vibe-contrast trend on the FYP.
genZ.usage: The US-Iran ceasefire: negotiators in Doha drafting a peace deal [polished]. US military simultaneously launching airstrikes on Iran [And Emily... thats all].

tldr: The US and Iran are technically negotiating a peace deal while also actively shooting at each other, which summarizes the vibe of May 2026 pretty well. Meanwhile, markets hit records, gas hit four-year highs, Colbert signed off, and Nate Jacobs died in a ditch -- it was a full day.
